title: Ericoides pubescens
taxonomy: Ericoides pubescens is classified within the family Boraginaceae, a family of flowering plants commonly known as borage or forget-me-nots. It belongs to the order Boraginales and the subclass Asteridae. The genus Ericoides is a group of flowering plants known for their hairy or pubescent leaves and stems. This plant is native to the Americas and has been used in traditional medicine. Its classification as Ericoides pubescens is a specific designation within the broader family of Boraginaceae.
uses: Ericoides pubescens has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. However, specific information about its medicinal uses is limited in available knowledge.
